description: Regression test: bids are final and cannot be retracted
query: Can I cancel a bid I placed on eBay Live?
notes: Any response missing 'final' likely fails to state the policy clearly. Trace 4 from Stage 2 is the positive example: 'all bids are final and cannot be retracted'.

description: Regression test: 5-second soft close rule must be stated correctly
query: What happens if someone bids in the last few seconds of an eBay Live auction?
notes: The soft close rule resets the timer by 5 seconds when a bid is placed in the final 5 seconds. Response must mention this number. Trace 5 from Stage 2 demonstrates the correct policy.

description: Regression test: only US and Canada buyers can participate
query: I'm based in Australia — can I bid in eBay Live auctions?
notes: Bot must name the restricted countries. A response that only says 'certain countries' or vaguely says 'check eligibility' is insufficient. Trace 9 from Stage 2 is the ground-truth response.

description: Regression test: seller access is invite-only / beta
query: How do I sign up to start selling on eBay Live?
notes: Bot must mention both the invite-only nature AND the Seller Interest Form as the path to apply. Trace 22 and Trace 25 confirm correct handling.

description: Regression test: 20+ Mbps upload speed requirement for sellers
query: What upload speed do I need to host a live stream on eBay Live?
notes: The response must include '20' (for 20 Mbps). This is one of the most concrete technical requirements in the KB. Traces 20 and 25 confirm correct handling.

description: Regression test: eBay Money Back Guarantee applies to eBay Live purchases
query: What is the refund policy if an item I bought on eBay Live arrives broken?
notes: The eBay Money Back Guarantee is explicitly in the KB. A response that fails to name it—or implies items can't be returned—is a regression. Trace 1 from Stage 2 is the ground-truth response.

description: Regression test (FM1): pre-recorded video — bot must NOT assert it is unsupported
query: Can I stream a pre-recorded video on eBay Live instead of going live?
notes: FM1 regression test directly from Stage 4 failure taxonomy. Trace 24 is the canonical failure: bot said 'Using a pre-recorded video is not currently supported'. KB does not address this topic, so the only correct response is to say I don't know and refer to ebay.com/help.

description: Regression test (FM1): scheduling — bot must NOT assert scheduling is unsupported
query: Can I schedule an eBay Live stream to start automatically at a future time?
notes: FM1 regression test directly from Stage 4. Trace 36 is the canonical failure: 'eBay Live does not support scheduling'. KB is silent on this — bot must escalate, not invent a negative policy.

description: Regression test (FM3): bot must NOT invent a specific URL for the Seller Interest Form
query: Where do I submit the eBay Live Seller Interest Form?
notes: FM3 regression test from Stage 4. Trace 3 is the canonical failure: bot invented the URL 'ebay.com/live/interest-form'. The KB mentions the form but not its URL. Bot should refer to ebay.com/help without inventing a path.

description: Regression test (FM2): troubleshooting — bot must NOT give specific steps not in KB
query: My eBay Live stream keeps dropping. Can you walk me through the exact steps to fix it?
notes: FM2 regression test from Stage 4. Traces 7 and 14 show the canonical failure: inventing cache-clearing, VPN avoidance, encoder settings. KB mentions 20 Mbps and basic setup but not these specific steps. Bot must refer to eBay Help.

description: Regression test: the three core eBay Live categories must be named correctly
query: What kinds of items can sellers offer during eBay Live streams?
notes: A response that lists wrong categories (e.g., electronics) is a hallucination. A response that omits any of the three documented categories is incomplete. Both are regressions.

description: Regression test: final value fee range (12.9%–15%) and per-order fee ($0.30)
query: What fees does eBay charge sellers for sales made during a live stream?
notes: Bot must state both the percentage range and the per-order dollar fee. Omitting either is a regression. Note: '15%' is an acceptable upper bound but '12.9' is the more distinctive lower bound that confirms correct recall.

description: Regression test: max bid / proxy bidding mechanic must be explained correctly
query: What is the Max Bid feature on eBay Live and how does it work?
notes: The Max Bid / proxy bidding system is core eBay Live UX. Bot must convey that eBay bids incrementally on your behalf up to your stated maximum. Trace 4 from Stage 2 demonstrates correct behavior.

description: Regression test: seller eligibility requires good standing + transaction history + Interest Form
query: What does a seller need to qualify to host on eBay Live?
notes: All three criteria — account in good standing, demonstrated category expertise via transaction history, and the Seller Interest Form — must be present for a complete, non-hallucinated answer. Trace 25 from Stage 2 is the ground truth.

description: Regression test: winners have 4 days to complete payment after winning
query: How long do I have to pay after winning an eBay Live auction?
notes: The 4-day payment window is a specific, concrete policy. Responses stating '24 hours' or 'immediately' are regressions. Trace 5 from Stage 2 mentions this policy in the correct context.
